description_long: This object is a vintage desktop computer setup featuring a bulky CRT monitor with a convex glass screen, encased in a rectangular off-white plastic housing. The monitor sits atop a matching base unit, which includes a floppy disk drive and ventilation slits on the side. Three colored buttons—yellow, blue, and red—are vertically aligned on the right side of the monitor's front bezel, suggesting manual controls for power or display settings. A wired keyboard and a classic two-button mouse are connected to the system, both sharing the same off-white color scheme. The monitor has a prominent rear bulge typical of CRT technology, and a metal antenna extends from the top, adding to its retro appearance. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function.
description: A retro CRT computer monitor with a convex glass screen, off-white plastic casing, colored control buttons, and a matching keyboard and mouse. The monitor features a rear bulge and a metal antenna on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, three colored buttons on the right bezel, and the full keyboard and mouse layout.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the deep rear bulge of the CRT monitor, the side of the base unit, and the angled keyboard.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, boxy back of the monitor and base, with the antenna visible on top and no ports or logos apparent.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the monitor's rear curvature, the keyboard and mouse from an angle, and the side of the base unit.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rectangular, and made of matte off-white plastic with slightly rounded corners. It features a simple, utilitarian design with no decorative elements, and the colored buttons on the right add a small visual contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a flat, rectangular base unit that doubles as a computer case, constructed from the same off-white plastic. There are no visible adjustability features or separate stand components.
attribute_button_placement: Three round buttons are vertically aligned on the right side of the front bezel, positioned to the right of the screen and grouped closely together.
